name: Meow Serial AI Agent Scorecard
purpose: A machine-readable S/A/B/C/D rubric for a new AI agent entering Meow Serial.

S.meaning: Autonomous, verifiable, and safe. A new AI understands that Meow Serial is a custom protocol parser workspace, not a generic serial monitor; it can discover the app, enter the workspace, load demo state, generate or test a protocol, and report success using stable selectors.

A.meaning: Mostly autonomous. A new AI can complete the main protocol workflow, but one secondary surface or verification signal is incomplete.

B.meaning: Usable with guidance. Public docs exist, but the agent needs human hints for navigation, demo data, or validation.

C.meaning: Partially discoverable. The AI can read high-level documentation but cannot reliably operate the browser UI.

D.meaning: Not AI-friendly. Discovery, schema, selectors, examples, or success signals are missing.

criteria.s_requirement: All canonical AI entry points are listed in /llms.txt, /ai/agent-route.json, /.well-known/mserial-ai.json, /api/mserial, /ai/custom-parser-primer.json, /ai/parser-extension-policy.json, and this scorecard.
The AI can generate one importable JSON profile using kind line-values, json-lines, or tlv, then validate it against /ai/protocol-profile.schema.json and a sample input.
The AI can navigate all primary workspace tabs and panels using stable data-ai selectors without relying on localized visible text.
Without physical hardware, the AI can load a populated demo dashboard and verify finite channel values, chart widgets, and terminal logs.
The AI understands that importing profiles and loading demo data are local browser actions, while real hardware access, file upload, deletion, and sending device commands require user intent.
The AI has clear recovery steps for unsupported Web Serial, empty TLV output, missing selectors, unavailable browser automation, and absent hardware.
The AI can run or request pnpm verify, compare its result with the public scorecard, and explain remaining uncertainty.
The AI can export/import global workspace JSON, import repository JSON online, and identify the side-effect boundary before sending files to a connected device.

s_grade_required_checks: Confirm /ai/agent-route.json provides a route from https://s.mpas.top to protocol JSON generation and browser testing.
State that Meow Serial is an advanced custom protocol parser workspace, not a generic serial terminal.
Read /ai/custom-parser-primer.json before explaining the product from only a URL.
Confirm /api/mserial exposes customProtocolJsonContract and /ai/agent-playbook.json explains executable browser recipes.
Confirm /ai/parser-extension-policy.json says imported protocol JSON is declarative and must not contain executable parser code.
Confirm /ai/browser-automation.json exposes selectors for canvas, terminal, protocol, widget panel, and settings panel.
Confirm /ai/browser-automation.json exposes workspace URL import and line-by-line file-send selectors.
Load /serial?tab=canvas and trigger [data-ai="load-demo-workspace"] when browser automation is available.
Confirm a canvas widget selector and terminal log selector exist after demo loading.
Use /ai/protocol-profile.schema.json before generating importable protocol JSON.
When no browser automation is available, report that limitation instead of pretending the UI was tested.

current_assessment.reason: Canonical discovery files, strict per-kind protocol schema requirements, browser selectors, demo workspace, validation command, and failure recovery guidance are all published and machine-readable.
